_comment: AUTOSAR MUD fixed vocabulary catalog — injected into every generation prompt

memory_sections..VAR_NOINIT: RAM variables not initialised on reset (persists across resets)
memory_sections..VAR_INIT: RAM variables initialised to zero on startup
memory_sections..VAR_POWER_ON_INIT: Variables initialised once at power-on only

autosar_concepts.ComplexDeviceDriverSWC: SWC accessing hardware ECU abstraction directly
autosar_concepts.SenderReceiverInterface: Asynchronous data exchange between SWCs (ports: RP_/PP_)
autosar_concepts.CalibrationParameter: Read-only parameter configurable at key-on; accessed via Rte_Prm_()
autosar_concepts.InterRunnableVariable: Shared memory between runnables in same SWC; accessed via Rte_IrvRead/Write()
autosar_concepts.ExclusiveArea: Critical section guard for IRVs shared across OS tasks
autosar_concepts.DEM: Diagnostic Event Manager — reports and stores fault events
autosar_concepts.FiM: Function Inhibition Manager — gates runnable execution on fault state
autosar_concepts.WdgM: Watchdog Manager — alive/deadline supervision of runnables

port_naming.PP_: Provided Port (this SWC writes / provides data or service)
port_naming.RP_: Required Port (this SWC reads / consumes data or service)

interface_naming.IF_SR_: Sender-Receiver interface for cyclic data signals

rte_api.Rte_Read(port, &var): Read from Required SR port into local variable
rte_api.Rte_Write(port, value): Write value to Provided SR port
rte_api.Rte_IRead(port): Implicit read — returns value directly (no pointer)

fim_api.FiM_GetFunctionPermission(FID, &permitte: Check if function is permitted (not inhibited)

wdgm_api.WdgM_UpdateAliveCounter(SupervisedEntity: Report runnable alive to watchdog manager

safe_state_reactions.generic_output: Write fail-safe default value to PP_ port
safe_state_reactions.init_fail: Write INIT_FAIL status, set module state = ERROR

runnable_naming.RE_Init: Initialisation runnable — triggered once at startup
runnable_naming.RE_Cyclic_Xms: Periodic cyclic runnable — X = period in ms

DriverTorque.error_handling: Dual-channel plausibility; NaN check; gradient limit 50 Nm/s

AssistTorque.safe_state: Force to 0.0 Nm if FaultFlag=1; ramp-down within 200ms

few_shot_runnable_pseudocode.RE_ControlTorque_example:
// [1] Guard: mode check
IF eps_state != ASSIST_ACTIVE:
    Rte_IWrite(PP_AssistTorque, 0.0)
    RETURN

// [2] Read inputs via RTE
speed  = Rte_IRead(RP_VehicleSpeed_DE_VehicleSpeed)
torque = Rte_IRead(RP_DriverTorque_DE_DriverTorque)
fault  = Rte_IrvRead(irv_FaultFlag)

// [3] Fault gate
IF fault == TRUE:
    Rte_IWrite(PP_AssistTorque, 0.0)
    RETURN

// [4] Compute assist factor
assist_factor = Interpolate(SpeedAssistCurve, speed)
Rte_IrvWrite(irv_AssistFactor, assist_factor)

// [5] Compute and clamp output torque
raw_torque = torque * assist_factor
assist_cmd = clamp(raw_torque, -Rte_Prm(RP_CalPrm_MaxTorque), Rte_Prm(RP_CalPrm_MaxTorque))
Rte_IWrite(PP_AssistTorque, assist_cmd)

// [6] Report alive to WdgM
WdgM_UpdateAliveCounter(WDGM_SE_CONTROL_TORQUE)

MAX_TORQUE.notes: OEM-configurable; loaded from NvRAM during RE_Init

SpeedAssistCurve.notes: Linearly interpolated; factor must be monotonically decreasing with speed
